title: Dramatic Smoky Tunisian Lamb Tagine
description: This earthy and snappy dish is a classic Tunisian tagine featuring lamb, soy sauce, and a blend of spices, with a smoky flavor from the use of smoked paprika, and is tamasic friendly from an ayurvedic perspective.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the lamb and cook until it is browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper, if using, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the soy sauce and 1 cup of water, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Cover the tagine or Dutch oven and transfer it to the oven, where it will cook for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Remove the tagine from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
